title: The Alley
narrativeGoal: Marcus closes the bar and discovers someone has been watching. The physical threat makes Elena's story real — this isn't journalism, it's survival. Marcus's old instincts surface against his will.
emotionalBeat: Controlled fear crystallizing into cold alertness
readerEffect: The world outside the bar is dangerous. Marcus is not as retired as he claims.

knows: Someone is following Marcus or Elena
Marcus's body still remembers how to fight
The threat is physical, not abstract
suspects: The watcher is connected to Elena's investigation
Marcus recognized something about the watcher

characterKnowledgeChanges.marcus: Realizes the threat Elena described is real and already here

subtext.surfaceConversation: Marcus closing up for the night, routine movements
subtext.actualConversation: Marcus's body language reveals his training — he's sweeping sightlines, checking exits, all while pretending to mop
subtext.enforcementRule: Marcus never thinks 'I'm in danger.' His body acts; his mind narrates the routine.

marcus: Minimal speech — talks to himself in fragments if at all
Physical actions carry all the narrative weight

pacing: Methodical routine that slowly tightens. Every mundane action becomes loaded once the watcher is noticed.
sensoryNotes: Night sounds. The difference between bar sounds and street sounds. Fluorescent back-alley light vs. amber bar light. Cold air hitting warm skin.
sceneSpecificProhibitions: No dialogue with other characters — Marcus is alone
No naming the emotion of fear — show it through physicality only
No action-movie choreography — this is one moment of seeing and being seen

anchorLines.text: He locked the front door the way he always did. Deadbolt, chain, handle check. Except tonight his hands did it in the wrong order.
anchorLines.placement: Opening — the first crack in the routine

chunkDescriptions: Closing routine — methodical, sensory, establishing the quiet after Elena's visit. Something feels off but Marcus can't name it yet.
The watcher — Marcus sees the figure. His body responds before his mind catches up. Scene ends on the alley going quiet.
failureModeToAvoid: Turning this into an action scene. The tension comes from stillness and recognition, not from confrontation.
